data.title: AbbVie sees negative Q3, full-year impact from IPR&D and milestones expense
data.description:
• AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) said it will incur a negative impact of $1.50 to GAAP and non-GAAP diluted earnings per share in Q3 due to acquired IPR&D and milestones expense of $2.7B.
  • As a result, the company now sees Q3 adjusted diluted earnings per share of $1.74-$1.78. Consensus is $3.27.
  • AbbVie also said that its full-year EPS guidance range is now $10.38-$10.58. Consensus is $11.97.

data.title: Azitra receives notice of non-compliance from NYSE American
data.description:
• Azitra (NYSE:AZTR) on Friday said that it received a notice from the staff of NYSE American LLC  that it was not in compliance with the exchange’s continued listing standards under Section 1003(a)(ii) of the NYSE American Company Guide.
  • The company was found to have insufficient stockholders' equity, failing to meet the required $4 million due to reported losses in three of its last four fiscal years.
  • Azitra has until October 31, 2025, to submit a plan outlining actions to regain compliance by April 1, 2027.
  • The company is exploring multiple funding options and intends to submit the plan on time.

  • The notice does not impact the trading of Azitra’s common stock or its operations.

  • AZTR -2.81% after hours to $0.7692.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: vTv Therapeutics registers 15.9M Class A shares for potential resale
data.description:
• vTv Therapeutics (VTV) registered 15.88M Class A common shares for resale by selling stockholders.
  • Includes 2.13M outstanding shares, plus shares issuable from warrants: 8.47M shares via pre-funded warrants and 5.27M shares via common warrants.
  • Shares originated from letter agreements, Class B to Class A conversions, and private placements in February 2024 and September 2025.
  • Warrants were also issued through these agreements and private placements.
  • vTv will not receive proceeds from resale by Selling Stockholders.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Chevron puts D-J Basin pipeline assets up for sale for more than $2B - Reuters
data.description:
Chevron (NYSE:CVX) is seeking to sell a collection of pipeline assets in the Denver-Julesburg Basin that are expected to fetch more than $2 billion, Reuters reported Friday.

Bank of America has been working to solicit potential interest in the assets, which were largely inherited from Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) acquisition of Noble Energy in 2020 and full takeover of Noble's midstream business a year later, according to the report.

Chevron (CVX) is one of the largest oil and gas producers in the D-J Basin, which mostly covers Colorado but also parts of Wyoming.

The company has been carrying out plans to cut as much as 20% of its global workforce, as it copes with how to control costs, compete with rivals, and maintain financial performance, all during an uncertain oil price outlook.

data.title: Roivant Sciences files shelf registration for potential securities offerings
data.description:
• Roivant Sciences (NASDAQ:ROIV) may offer common shares, preference shares, debt securities, warrants, and units from time to time.
  • The offerings may be made by the company or selling securityholders, depending on the terms.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Hertz promotes Mike Moore to Chief Operating Officer
data.description:
• Hertz Global Holdings (HTZ) has promoted Mike Moore to Chief Operating Officer, with full responsibility for the company’s fleet operations.

  • Moore, who joined Hertz in July 2024 as Executive Vice President of Technical Operations, brings over 25 years of experience in fleet operations and maintenance.
  • Prior to Hertz, he held leadership roles at Delta Air Lines, Northwest Airlines, and Virgin Galactic.
  • HTZWW +0.57% after hours to $3.48.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Union Pacific locomotive engineers ratify new contract with pay hikes, stronger benefits
data.description:
• The 6,000 members of the locomotive engineers union have agreed to ratify a five-year contract with Union Pacific Corp (NYSE:UNP) providing for higher wages, improved health and welfare benefits, and accelerated vacation accrual policies.
  • Among the terms of the new contract, Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) engineers will receive raises of 18.8% over the life of the agreement and preserves the 11/4 work-rest agreement which provides 4 days off after 11 consecutive working days.
  • The contract also increases the allowance for meals away from home, an optional, reduced-cost medical plan, and caps on monthly cost-sharing for new and existing medical coverage.
  • Since the beginning of the year, over 12,600 Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) employees have ratified new contracts.

data.title: ProFrac Holding files to sell 3.17M shares of Class A common stock for holders
data.description:
• ProFrac Holding (NASDAQ:ACDC) filed a prospectus for the offer and sale of 3.17M shares of Class A common stock by the selling stockholders. 
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities. 
  •  

Filing.

data.title: Jaguar Health registers 2.62M shares for resale following private placements
data.description:
• Jaguar Health (NASDAQ:JAGX) registered 2.62M shares of common stock for resale by existing stockholders.
  • Includes 1.98M shares issuable upon exchange of 950.8 shares of Series N Preferred Stock.
  • The Series N Preferred Stock was issued in a private placement closed on September 10, 2025.
  • Also includes 161,583 shares of common stock and 479,442 shares issuable via a pre-funded warrant.
  • These additional shares stem from a second private placement closed on September 28, 2025.
  • All shares may be resold by the participating selling stockholders, not the company itself.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico reports September passenger traffic increase of 1%
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico (NYSE:PAC) on Friday said the total number of terminal passengers at GAP’s 12 Mexican airports increased by 1% in September 2025, compared to September 2024.
  • Puerto Vallarta and Guadalajara airports presented an increase in passenger traffic of 2.6% and 0.3%, respectively, while Los Cabos, Tijuana and Montego Bay airports decreased 4.8%, 0.6%, and 0.3%, respectively, compared to September 2024.
  • The seats available during September 2025 increased by 1.3%, compared to September 2024.
  • The load factors for the month went from 81.0% in September 2024 to 80.7% in September 2025.
  • Source: Press Release
